findings.title: Train/val/test PDDL problems absent from repo; only an inaccessible anonymized URL
findings.claim: All training, validation, and test PDDL instances live in a separate custom pddlgym fork; the repo itself contains no .pddl domain or problem files (check_repo_completeness.py: pddl_problem_files_in_repo=0), and the only pointer is an anonymous.4open.science link that returns HTTP 401.
findings.concern: Every headline number (Tables 2/4/5/6) is computed over these instances, but the dataset defining train/val/test is not in the repo and the linked source is unreachable, so none of the results can be reproduced.
findings.resolution: Authors: provide the custom pddlgym domains/problems (or the supplementary dataset) at a resolvable, non-anonymized location and pin which problem indices form each easy/medium/hard test subset.
findings.paper_ref: Appendix A.2 footnote 'Dataset submitted as supplementary material'; Table 1
findings.reason: README lines 23-26 match the quote exactly; verifier script (pddl_problem_files_in_repo=0) and live HTTP probe (HTTP 401 after redirect) both confirm the claim.

findings.title: No dependency-specification file; deps only as prose with several unpinned packages
findings.quote:
pip install numpy==1.26.0
pip install protobuf==3.20.0
pip3 install torch torchvision torchaudio --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu126
pip install torch-geometric==2.6.1
pip install torch-scatter torch-sparse torch-cluster -f https://data.pyg.org/whl/torch-2.6.0+cu126.html
pip install tensorboard
pip install torchviz==0.0.2
pip install pytorch-lightning==2.0.1
pip install wandb
pip install icecream
pip install pymimir==0.9.71
pip install pyperplan==2.1
pip install pandas
findings.claim: There is no requirements.txt / setup.py / environment.yml / pyproject.toml in the repo (check_repo_completeness.py: dependency_spec_file=0); dependencies are listed only as README pip commands, and torch/torchvision/torchaudio, tensorboard, wandb, icecream, pandas, termcolor, tarski, and clingo are unpinned.
findings.concern: Without a pinned, machine-readable environment the build is not deterministically reproducible (e.g. an unpinned torch against the pinned torch-geometric 2.6.1 / torch-2.6.0 wheels can break).
findings.resolution: Authors: add a requirements.txt or environment.yml with fully pinned versions for every dependency, including torch.
findings.reason: README lines 3-21 match the quote exactly; verifier script confirms dependency_spec_file=0 and no requirements.txt/setup.py/environment.yml/pyproject.toml present; unpinned packages (torch, tensorboard, wandb, pandas, etc.) verified in the README.

findings.title: No code for the LLM comparison (Table 5 / Appendix A.3, OpenAI-O3 & Gemini-2.5-Pro)
findings.quote:
To evaluate the relative capabilities of our approach against state-of-the-art language models, we
conducted experiments using OpenAI's O3 model and Gemini-2.5-Pro (both released in 2025).
Following the methodology of [28], we adopted their One-Shot prompting technique to generate
prompts for our planning problems.
findings.claim: Appendix A.3 / Table 5 report a GABAR-vs-LLM comparison, but no script in the repo generates the one-shot prompts, queries OpenAI-O3 or Gemini-2.5-Pro, or validates the returned plans (grep for openai/gemini/gpt/one_shot/prompt across all .py returns nothing outside baselines).
findings.concern: The OpenAI-O3 and Gemini-2.5-Pro Coverage/PQR columns in Table 5 cannot be reproduced or audited; commercial-API nondeterminism makes this comparison unreproducible without the prompting/validation code and run logs.
findings.resolution: Authors: release the prompt-generation, model-query, and plan-validation scripts plus the raw model responses used for Table 5, and state model snapshot dates and decoding parameters.
findings.reason: Paper quote confirmed at paper_text.txt lines 1283-1285; grep for openai/gemini/gpt/one_shot across all .py files returns zero matches, confirming no LLM comparison code exists in the repo.

findings.title: No trained model weights or cached training-data shipped
findings.quote:
#### Testing a Pre-trained Model

```sh
python main.py --method ltp --domain manyblocks_ipcc_big --all-problems --num-test-problems 10 --mode test --max-plan-length 500 --run-learned-model True --use-global-node True
```
findings.claim: The README documents a 'Testing a Pre-trained Model' workflow, but no .pt/.pth/.ckpt weights and no training-data .pkl caches exist in the repo (check_repo_completeness.py: trained_weight_files=0, training_data_pickles=0); only the cache/results/.gitkeep skeleton ships.
findings.concern: Reproducing any number requires retraining from scratch, which is itself blocked by the missing dataset (see missing-pddl-problems) and the undisclosed sweep (see undisclosed-hparam-sweep).
findings.resolution: Authors: ship the per-domain best-model checkpoints (and the model_tracking.json) used to produce Table 2.
findings.reason: README lines 105-109 match the quote exactly; verifier script confirms trained_weight_files=0 and training_data_pickles=0; 'Testing a Pre-trained Model' workflow is documented but unrunnable without weights.

findings.title: Runner sweeps n_heads and attention-dropout; paper describes a single fixed config
findings.quote:
METHOD="ltp"
heads=(1 2 4 8)
lrs=(0.0005)
decays=(0.000)
other_drops=(0)
gnn_rounds=(9)
findings.claim: The canonical runner trains+tests GABAR across n_heads in {1,2,4,8} and (for ablation 'main', line 175) attention-dropout in {0.1,0.2}, logging each run to a separate file; analyse_domain_results.py then aggregates all logs into a CSV (check_selection_on_test.py confirms heads_swept='1 2 4 8', attn_drops_for_main='0.1 0.2').
findings.concern: The paper's Training Procedure (§3.4) specifies a single fixed configuration (lr 0.0005, 9 GNN rounds, batch 16, hidden 64) and never mentions tuning the number of attention heads or attention dropout, so the reported numbers may be the best of an undisclosed sweep.
findings.resolution: Authors: state the n_heads and attention-dropout actually used per domain and how they were selected; if a sweep was run, report the selection criterion and the full grid.
findings.reason: ltp_all_run.sh lines 57-61 match the quote exactly; line 175 confirms attn_drops=(0.1 0.2) for ablation 'main'; verifier script output confirms heads_swept='1 2 4 8' and attn_drops_for_main='0.1 0.2', none of which is disclosed in the paper's §3.4 single-config description.

findings.title: Reported checkpoint chosen by best TEST-set coverage, not validation loss as paper claims
findings.quote:
# Track best model for this planner type
if metrics.success_rate_with_monitor > best_results[planner_type]["success_rate"]:
    best_results[planner_type]["success_rate"] = metrics.success_rate_with_monitor
    best_results[planner_type]["model_type"] = model_type
    best_results[planner_type]["epoch"] = epoch
findings.claim: main.py evaluates up to 6 checkpoints per run on the test set (num_models_to_test=2 for each of all_model_types=['validation','training','combined']) and log_model_metrics then reports, as 'Best Model', the checkpoint with the highest success_rate_with_monitor, which is the test-set coverage (check_selection_on_test.py confirms the active definition selects by 'success_rate_with_monitor (TEST coverage)').
findings.concern: Selecting which checkpoint/run to report by its score on the test set is test-set leakage into model selection and contradicts the paper's stated criterion 'we select the model checkpoint that achieves the lowest loss on the validation set', biasing the reported Coverage/PQR upward.
findings.resolution: Authors: confirm whether the Table 2 numbers come from the validation-loss checkpoint or from this best-by-test-coverage selection; if the latter, re-report using only the validation-selected checkpoint (and validation-selected hyperparameters).
findings.paper_ref: Section 3.4: 'we select the model checkpoint that achieves the lowest loss on the validation set for evaluation'
findings.reason: test_utils.py lines 391-395 match the quote exactly; main.py lines 903/911 confirm all_model_types=['validation','training','combined'] each tested via test_function_v2 (test set problems); verifier script confirms active_log_model_metrics_selects_by='success_rate_with_monitor (TEST coverage)'; the contradiction with §3.4's stated validation-loss criterion is substantiated.
